channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-22
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `Davidvandijcke/coarse` project has made strides in localization and deployment, with fixes for confirmation emails, review pages, and schema migrations. Meanwhile, `JamieCummins/regcheck` has focused on enhancing security features and addressing bugs in OSF and mutlistudy, demonstrating a commitment to robustness and reliability. In related news, `giladfeldman/escicheck` has synchronized its `main` branch with the `escicheckapp` repository, ensuring compatibility with the latest versions of `effectcheck`. These updates highlight the interconnected nature of our projects and the community's dedication to advancing open-source research tools. Overall, it's an exciting time for our community, with collaborations and innovations driving progress across the board.

104 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck32 commits
coarse30 commits
docpluck28 commits
metacheck10 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch was worked on, where documentation was added for "RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97)" in the learnings section. The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch also saw fixes to tables, specifically addressing "DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress" in version 2.4.97. Additionally, on the `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch, a rendering fix was made to "strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose" in version 2.4.96, related to RC-T.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 3e9cc2c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(learnings): RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97) — Gilad Feldman
  • 813aa4c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress — Gilad Feldman
  • 84a4d42 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of recent work, with a commit fixing OSF and mutlistudy bugs, as stated in the message "fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs". Another commit on the staging branch aimed to "harden security features", enhancing the repository's security. No other branches were worked on during this period, with all changes confined to the staging branch. The fixes and security hardening were both done by Jamie Cummins. The specific bugs fixed in OSF and mutlistudy are not detailed, but they were addressed in the first commit. The security features that were hardened are also not specified, but the intention was to increase security.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, presumably to make it easier for users to access or install the OIF extension from the Chrome Webstore. The commit does not mention any other changes or additions beyond the Chrome Webstore link. There were no other commits to any other branches, so the rest of the repository remains unchanged.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
